data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-22T00:42:54.597Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 8's **`ExecuteUpdateAsync`** and **`ExecuteDeleteAsync`** — they translate directly to SQL `UPDATE`/`DELETE` statements, bypassing entity tracking entirely.

```csharp
// Operation 1: single SQL UPDATE — no entities loaded
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s =>
        s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: single SQL DELETE — no entities loaded
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Why this is faster:**

| | Original | Bulk API |
|---|---|---|
| SQL roundtrips | Load all rows + SaveChanges | 1 statement each |
| Memory | All entities tracked | Zero entities loaded |
| Change tracker overhead | Per-entity diff | None |

**Caveats:**
- These bypass the change tracker — any already-loaded entities won't reflect the changes. Call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if needed.
- No `SaveChangesAsync()` call required — they execute immediately.
- EF Core interceptors/`SaveChanges` hooks won't fire; use SQL triggers or call these outside tracked workflows.
